title: MAP Quest Type feature or app to get miles
excerpt: We track mile from our warehouses across the country to job sites. If there was an app that could calculate the miles for us and store the milage it would be a good feature. We then could calculate a delivery charge.

posts.author: tmac (Thomas MacDonald)
posts.cookedHtml: We track mile from our warehouses across the country to job sites. If there was an app that could calculate the miles for us and store the milage it would be a good feature. We then could calculate a delivery charge.

posts.author: Laz (Lazaro Diaz)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve also been looking for this functionality. Right now we run a google maps search for each route and hard key the distance for the shortest route into a numbers column. Not impossible but just boring busy work that we would like to automate.

Thanks

Laz

posts.author: tmac (Thomas MacDonald)
posts.cookedHtml: We do the same in my business. We have this process off Monday.com in a spreadsheet. We try to automate all our processes into mon.com . This would be the next step for us.

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@tmac

Are you familiar with Integromat? If not, its a 3rd party integration tool where you can connect other apps to Monday and create your own custom integrations.

I haven’t actually used Google Maps in Integromat yet, but looking at this module:

I’m imagining you can set up a scenario in Integromat to pick up an address from Monday, run it against your origin and have it output the mileage to a column on your board.

Let me know if you need help setting up Integromat.

posts.author: Laz (Lazaro Diaz)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @andrewalmand we use Zapier. Is that similar/same?

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Laz

Correct Zapier and Integromat are the same type of software. I don’t use Zapier though so I can’t speak to the integrations it offers. I would start there and see what kind of map applications you can connect.

posts.author: Laz (Lazaro Diaz)
posts.cookedHtml:
@andrewalmand thank you!

@PolishedGeek is this something you’d be able to sort out for us?

Thank you!

-Laz

posts.author: SAVE22.vet (Jason)
posts.cookedHtml: have you been able to figure this out yet?

posts.author: Krunchy (Guillaume Servonnat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Me too
I need to be able to get a distance from a departure to a destination.
That would be awesome to have an api doing this

posts.author: LHebard (Laura S)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a very helpful tool for our team to use as well. Currently we are using Mile IQ to help track miles of field technicians. It would be very helpful if Monday could assemble a route based on assignments and supply a minimum number of miles for their selected assignments in a given time period, to compare to invoices.

posts.author: Krunchy (Guillaume Servonnat)
posts.cookedHtml: FYI I’ve been able to do this type of calculation using Google Maps api and Zapier, and that works fine !

posts.author: MCautreels (Maarten Cautreels - Tryve)
posts.cookedHtml:
For those not into Zapier or Make, we’ve build an app called Location Toolkit on the monday.com Marketplace that allows you to calculate both travel distance as well as time between two Location columns within monday.com.

Check it out with a 14 day free trial and get in touch if you have any questions!

Best,

Maarten

posts.author: karin1 (Karin Lualhati)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Laura,

Were you able to find a way to integrate Mile IQ to Monday?
Our company is also using the same tool and wondering if it is possible to integrate into Monday.
